prompt: What does the context window measure, and what does it include?
choices: The maximum tokens the model can consider at once, covering both input and output
Only the number of input tokens you are allowed to send
The model's permanent long-term memory of all past refactors
The maximum number of separate API calls allowed per minute

prompt: During a long multi-file refactor, why must the AI tool re-supply the prior context on every call?
choices: Because each API call is stateless — the model retains no memory between calls
Because re-sending makes the model respond faster
Because the provider deletes old messages after one minute
It doesn't — the model automatically remembers all earlier files and edits

prompt: Which of the following are valid strategies for staying within the context window while working across a large codebase or a long requirements thread? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Summarizing an earlier interview or transcript into a short recap
Retrieving only the few files relevant to the current edit
Keeping only the most recent N turns (sliding window)
Increasing the temperature setting

prompt: An AI assistant doing a 30-file refactor starts ignoring a 'keep the old column name' rule it was given at the start. Which fact best explains the root cause to the team?
choices: The model's temperature is set too low
As the transcript grew past the window limit, the early rule was dropped and is no longer seen by the model
The model's training finished, so it stopped learning
Retrieval always corrupts the edit history

prompt: You need a model to work across a large codebase where exact details matter and files cross-reference each other. Summarize-then-work vs retrieve-relevant-chunks — which *trade-off* fits?
choices: Summarize the whole codebase once and work from the summary — it's the most compact
Retrieve the specific relevant chunks per question — a summary would lose the exact details you need
Paste the entire codebase every call so nothing is ever missed
Lower the temperature so the model holds more of it in mind
